engineIntelligence.h2: Subaru Outback Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Subaru Outback in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data.

cards.altText: Subaru Outback EE20 engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: DPF clogging - diesel particulate filter blockage common on short-journey vehicles, typically 50k-80k miles.
EGR valve failure - exhaust gas recirculation valve sticking causes rough idle and emissions faults.
Head gasket degradation - less common than EJ series but documented on high-mileage diesel boxer units.

cards.altText: Subaru Outback FB25 engine - petrol 2.5L replacement UK
knownFailures: Oil consumption - piston ring wear increases oil use on higher-mileage naturally aspirated units, typically 70k-100k miles.
Timing chain stretch - chain tensioner wear can cause rattling on engines over 90k miles.
Valve cover gasket leaks - common ageing issue on SOHC boxer configuration.

cards.altText: Subaru Outback EZ36 engine - petrol 3.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: Head gasket failure - documented across high-mileage six-cylinder boxer engines, typically 80k-120k miles.
Oil consumption - piston ring wear on older EZ36 units causes increased oil use.
Timing chain tensioner wear - can cause rattling noise on cold start after 90k miles.

engineGuide.h2: Subaru Outback Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The EE20 diesel boxer brought efficient turbodiesel power to UK Outback models, delivering strong torque for towing and motorway cruising.
commonFailures: DPF clogging - diesel particulate filter blockage common on short-journey vehicles, typically 50k-80k miles
EGR valve failure - exhaust gas recirculation valve sticking causes rough idle and emissions faults
Head gasket degradation - documented on high-mileage diesel boxer units, typically 80k-100k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for EE20 Subaru Outback engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel (Updated)
entries.history: The revised EE20Z variant featured updated EGR calibration and emissions components for improved Euro 6 compliance on later Outback models.
commonFailures: DPF regeneration issues - updated software required for proper regeneration cycles on Euro 6 models
EGR cooler leaks - documented on post-2015 units with high mileage
AdBlue system faults - SCR system calibration issues on later diesel variants

Timing chain stretch - chain tensioner wear can cause rattling on engines over 90k miles
Valve cover gasket leaks - common ageing issue on SOHC boxer configuration

commonProblems.h2: Subaru Outback Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Subaru Outback's EE20 diesel and FB/EZ series petrol boxer engines deliver capable performance but share documented failure points with other Subaru models. DPF/EGR issues dominate diesel replacement enquiries, followed by oil consumption concerns on FB25 petrol units and head gasket degradation on high-mileage six-cylinder EZ engines. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components addressing the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

problems.h4: DPF Clogging - Why Subaru Outback EE20 Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Diesel particulate filter becomes clogged with soot on vehicles used primarily for short journeys, preventing proper regeneration cycles and causing loss of power, warning lights, and eventual limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF, professional cleaning or replacement with reconditioned unit, reset ECU, test drive
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before engine damage; 40-60k mile expectation on cleaned DPF

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EE20 Diesel Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EE20 diesel engine with updated DPF components, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Long-term solution; 12-month unlimited warranty, addresses root cause and other wear items

problems.recommendation: On a Subaru Outback worth £4,000-£12,000, a full rebuilt EE20 diesel engine often represents better value than repeated DPF cleaning if the vehicle has high mileage or other wear issues. We recommend rebuilt units with updated emissions components to prevent repeat failure.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EE20 diesel engine prices for your Subaru Outback

problems.h4: Oil Consumption - High-Mileage FB25 Piston Ring Wear
problems.rootCause: Piston ring lands wear on ageing naturally aspirated boxer engines, allowing oil to pass into combustion chambers and burn, causing blue exhaust smoke and frequent top-ups.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip engine, replace piston rings, hone cylinders, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Complex labour; viable only if block/heads in excellent condition

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt FB25 engine with updated piston rings, MLS gaskets, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most cost-effective long-term solution; eliminates oil consumption root cause

problems.recommendation: For Outback owners experiencing excessive oil use beyond 70k miles, a rebuilt FB25 engine with updated piston rings typically offers better value than a ring-only repair, especially on modern boxer engines where labour costs are significant.
problems.cta: Get quotes for low-oil-consumption rebuilt Outback engines

problems.h4: Head Gasket Failure - Subaru Outback EZ Series Coolant/Oil Cross-Contamination
problems.rootCause: Original head gaskets degrade under heat cycles and six-cylinder boxer engine vibration, allowing coolant and oil to mix, causing overheating and potential engine seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove cylinder heads, replace gasket with upgraded MLS unit, reseal, test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if block/heads undamaged; 30-50k mile expectation on gasket

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt EZ Series Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt EZ30/EZ36 engine with MLS gaskets, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; addresses head gasket + common EZ failure points together

problems.recommendation: If your Outback 3.0R or 3.6R has high mileage or shows signs of coolant/oil mixing beyond the head gasket, a full rebuilt EZ series engine typically costs less than separate gasket repair + ancillary work. We recommend rebuilt units with upgraded MLS gaskets.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EZ36 engine prices for your Subaru Outback

problems.h4: Timing Chain Stretch - FB Series Chain Tensioner Wear
problems.rootCause: Timing chain tensioner wear allows chain slack, causing rattling noise on cold start, potential timing variation, and risk of catastrophic engine damage if chain skips teeth.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Timing Chain Kit Replacement Only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, replace chain, tensioners, guides, reset timing, test
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught early before engine damage; 60-80k mile expectation on new components

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt FB Series Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit complete rebuilt FB20/FB25 engine with new timing components, bench-tested, warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term value; addresses timing chain + common FB failure points together

problems.recommendation: If your Outback shows timing chain rattling beyond 90k miles, a full rebuilt FB series engine often costs less than separate chain repair + potential collateral damage. We recommend rebuilt units with upgraded tensioners.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt FB25 engine prices for your Subaru Outback

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Subaru Outback
finalCta.paragraph: DPF clogging, oil consumption, head gasket, or timing chain issues don't have to mean the end for your Outback. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Subaru Outback specialists who understand boxer engine compatibility across EE20, FB, and EZ series. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery options.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Subaru Outback Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Subaru Outback and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineSizes.h2: Subaru Outback Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Subaru Outback is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Subaru Outback 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre EE20 diesel boxer family powered UK Outback models from 2010, offering strong torque for towing and motorway cruising with improved fuel economy over petrol variants. This SOHC flat-four design with turbocharging and common-rail injection delivers capable performance, though DPF and EGR vigilance is essential for long-term reliability.
commonFailurePoints: DPF clogging on short-journey use; EGR valve sticking; head gasket degradation on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Subaru Outback 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Subaru Outback 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre FB20 naturally aspirated boxer replaced belt-driven EJ units from 2012, bringing chain-driven reliability and reduced maintenance for UK Outback owners seeking economical everyday capability.
commonFailurePoints: Oil consumption on higher-mileage units; timing chain tensioner wear; valve cover gasket leaks
items.cta: Compare Subaru Outback 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru Outback 2.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.5-litre FB25 naturally aspirated boxer became the volume petrol engine for UK Outback models from 2014, delivering increased displacement and torque for improved drivability while retaining boxer smoothness and AWD compatibility.
commonFailurePoints: Oil consumption on higher-mileage units; timing chain tensioner wear; head gasket degradation on high-mileage examples
items.cta: Compare Subaru Outback 2.5L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru Outback 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 3.0-litre EZ30 six-cylinder boxer defined early Outback performance variants from 2003, delivering refined power and smooth torque for towing and motorway cruising with distinctive six-cylinder character.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on high-mileage units; oil consumption on older examples; timing chain tensioner wear
items.cta: Compare Subaru Outback 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Subaru Outback 3.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The flagship 3.6-litre EZ36 six-cylinder boxer delivered premium performance for Outback 3.6R models from 2010, combining smooth power delivery with Subaru's symmetrical AWD capability for confident all-weather capability.
commonFailurePoints: Head gasket failure on high-mileage units; oil consumption on older examples; timing chain tensioner wear

engineYears.h2: Subaru Outback Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Subaru Outback's 2003-present production run, organised so UK owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt replacement prices.

years.description: The launch years introduced the EZ30 six-cylinder boxer to UK Outback models, establishing the refined performance variant that would define early Outback capability.
keyChanges: Subaru Outback launched for UK market with EZ30 naturally aspirated six-cylinder boxer
3.0R performance variant introduced with enhanced suspension and styling
Head gasket recall campaign issued for early EZ30 series units
Manual and automatic transmission options with different ECU mapping
knownFor: Smooth six-cylinder boxer performance for UK buyers
Head gasket vigilance essential from 80k miles onwards on EZ series
Strong compatibility with Legacy EZ30 donor engines for replacements
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2009 Subaru Outback replacement engines
years.ticker: Outback head gasket replacement • EZ30 six-cylinder supply UK • 3.0R boxer engine compatibility

years.preview: Second Generation Outback: EE20 Diesel & FB20 Introduction
years.description: The second generation brought the EE20 diesel boxer and FB20 chain-driven petrol to UK Outback models, improving reliability and reducing maintenance requirements.
keyChanges: All-new BR chassis platform for 2010 model year
EE20 2.0L diesel boxer introduced across diesel trims
FB20 2.0L naturally aspirated chain-driven boxer replaced belt-driven EJ units
EZ36 3.6L six-cylinder introduced for flagship 3.6R performance variant
knownFor: EE20 diesel delivers strong torque for towing and motorway cruising
FB20 chain-driven design reduces long-term maintenance costs
EZ36 six-cylinder offers flagship performance with smooth power delivery
years.cta: Get quotes for 2010-2014 Subaru Outback replacement engines
years.ticker: Outback EE20 DPF replacement • FB20 oil consumption fix • EZ36 head gasket upgrade

years.preview: Third Generation Outback: FB25 & EE20Z Refinements
years.description: The third generation brought refined FB25 petrol and updated EE20Z diesel engines to UK Outback models, with improved emissions compliance and reliability enhancements.
keyChanges: All-new BS chassis platform for 2014 model year
FB25 2.5L naturally aspirated boxer introduced as volume petrol engine
EE20Z updated diesel variant with revised EGR calibration for Euro 6
EZ36 six-cylinder continued for flagship 3.6R performance variant
knownFor: FB25 refinements improved oil consumption characteristics for UK road conditions
EE20Z Euro 6 compliance essential for ULEZ and clean air zone access
Low-mileage 2017-2019 examples command premium in UK enthusiast market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2014-2019 Subaru Outback replacement engines
years.ticker: Outback FB25 oil consumption • EE20Z AdBlue compatibility • 2019 Outback low mileage engine

years.preview: Present - Fourth Generation Outback: FB25 Continuity
years.description: The fourth generation continues the FB25 naturally aspirated boxer for UK Outback models, with updated calibrations and improved emissions compliance for modern requirements.
keyChanges: All-new BT chassis platform for 2019 model year
FB25 2.0L/2.5L naturally aspirated chain-driven boxer continued across petrol trims
Updated ECU software and emissions calibration for Euro 6d compliance
No diesel option offered in UK market from 2019 onwards
knownFor: FB25 delivers improved reliability with chain-driven design for daily drivers
Reduced maintenance requirements compared to belt-driven legacy engines
Modern examples still under warranty; replacement demand growing as mileage accumulates
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019+ Subaru Outback replacement engines
years.ticker: Outback FB25 oil consumption • Fourth gen boxer compatibility • 2022+ Outback replacement options

faq.h2: Subaru Outback Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Subaru Outback engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Subaru Outback engine replacements typically range from £1,200 for rebuilt FB20 units to £4,500 for rebuilt EZ36 six-cylinder engines, supply only. Supply & fit adds £550-£1,100 depending on location and garage rates.
keyPoints: Naturally aspirated FB20/FB25 rebuilt: £1,200-£2,800 supply only
Diesel EE20 rebuilt: £1,895-£3,800 supply only
Six-cylinder EZ36 rebuilt: £2,400-£4,500 supply only
All quotes include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Get your free Subaru Outback engine replacement quote today

items.question: Which engine code fits my Subaru Outback?
items.answer: Your Outback uses either FB20/FB25 (naturally aspirated petrol), EE20 (diesel) or EZ36 (six-cylinder petrol) depending on variant and year. Enter your registration or VIN above to instantly verify your exact engine code.
keyPoints: 2.0i/2.5i models (2012+): FB20/FB25 naturally aspirated
2.0D models (2010-2019): EE20/EE20Z turbocharged diesel
3.6R models (2010-2019): EZ36 six-cylinder naturally aspirated
DVLA lookup confirms exact code before ordering replacement
items.cta: Verify your Outback engine code with our free compatibility checker

items.question: What is the difference between used and reconditioned Outback engines?
items.answer: Used engines are tested second-hand units from breakers, while reconditioned engines are fully rebuilt with new bearings, seals and upgraded components. Reconditioned units include longer warranties and address common failure points.
keyPoints: Used: £895-£3,200, 6-month warranty typical, mileage varies
Reconditioned: £1,200-£4,500, 12-month unlimited warranty standard
Reconditioned includes upgraded rings and MLS head gaskets
Reconditioned recommended for high-mileage or failure-damaged units
items.cta: Compare used vs reconditioned Subaru Outback engine options

items.question: How long does Outback engine replacement take in the UK?
items.answer: Most Subaru Outback engines are dispatched next-day from UK stock; fitting takes 1-2 days at approved Subaru specialists. Total turnaround from quote to road-ready is typically 3-5 working days.
keyPoints: Engines in UK stock: dispatched next working day
Specialist fitting: 1-2 days at approved Subaru garage
Supply & fit bookings can often be scheduled within 48 hours
EE20/FB/EZ compatibility checks completed before dispatch
items.cta: Check live Subaru Outback engine availability and fitting slots

items.question: Do Outback replacement engines come with warranty in the UK?
items.answer: Yes, all Subaru Outback engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Diesel EE20 and six-cylinder EZ36 models may qualify for extended warranty upgrades.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Warranty covers parts and labour at any UK garage
Performance models eligible for extended coverage
Claim process managed directly through supplying specialist
items.cta: View warranty options for your Subaru Outback engine replacement

items.question: Can I fit a different FB or EZ engine code in my Outback?
items.answer: FB20 and FB25 are often interchangeable with ECU verification, but EE20 diesel and EZ36 six-cylinder require matching wiring and mounts. Use our compatibility checker or speak to a Subaru Outback specialist before ordering a different code.
keyPoints: FB20 and FB25 often interchangeable on naturally aspirated models
EE20 diesel requires matching ECU, wiring harness and fuel system
EZ36 six-cylinder requires specific mounts and AWD compatibility
Specialist advice recommended for cross-code swaps on grey imports
items.cta: Get expert advice on FB/EZ code compatibility for your Outback

items.question: Will a replacement Outback engine pass MOT emissions in the UK?
items.answer: Yes, all replacement engines supplied through EngineMarket comply with UK MOT emissions standards. Documentation is provided to support compliance testing if required.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines tested to meet original emissions specifications
Documentation provided for MOT compliance
EE20/FB/EZ series engines retain original emissions classification
Specialists assist with testing paperwork where needed
items.cta: Ensure your Outback replacement engine is MOT-compliant

items.question: How do I find an Outback engine fitting garage near me in the UK?
items.answer: Use our garage locator to find approved Subaru Outback engine specialists near your postcode. All fitters are vetted, experienced with boxer engines and recognise our network warranty.
keyPoints: Nationwide network of Subaru-approved fitting specialists
All garages experienced with EE20/FB/EZ boxer and AWD systems
Warranty recognised at any approved fitting location
Supply & fit quotes include labour at your chosen garage
items.cta: Find a Subaru Outback engine specialist near you today

items.question: What if my Outback engine replacement doesn't fit?
items.answer: All Subaru Outback engines come with a compatibility guarantee. If the engine doesn't fit your vehicle, we'll replace it with the correct unit or refund you in full, no questions asked.
keyPoints: Compatibility guarantee covers all engine code mismatches
Free replacement or full refund if fitment issues arise
Pre-dispatch compatibility checks minimise risk
Specialist support available throughout installation process
items.cta: Order your Outback engine with complete fitment confidence

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in my Subaru Outback in the UK?
items.answer: For most Outback owners, engine replacement costs £1,200-£4,500 versus £4,000-£15,000+ for a replacement vehicle. Rebuilt engines extend life with upgraded components and warranty protection.
keyPoints: Replacement cost typically far below vehicle market value
Upgraded components address original failure points
12-month warranty provides peace of mind for capable vehicles
Preserve a unique vehicle with strong enthusiast following
